feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Download Oracle 12c Installation Software
entry.content:
we are unable see Oracle 12c(12.1.0.2) installation  Software downloads.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SUM Queries
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there any way to find out what actually is copied to shadow system during upgrade? Some consultants say that data is copied whereas other say only structures are copied.

My query is:

1)Is data copied in shadow system or structures?

2)If data is copied, then is there any way to find out exactly what data is copied to shadow system?

 

Best Regards,

Darpan Kumar

entry.title: ATP Customizing Disappeared
entry.content:
Hi,

After an Upgrade with HANA 2022 some ATP Customizing Functions disappeared and i do not know where to find them. In older HANA Version you had the possibility to tick following options in this customizing step:

Sales- Basic Functions- Availability Check- Availability Check with ATP- Define Availability Check Group 

1. Total Sales

2. Total Deliver Reqs

3. Block with TrO Qties

4. no Pac.

5. Accumul.

6. RelChkPlan

Last 3 options can be found in the new (2022) customizing step Define Basic Availability Check Group Behavior but i cannot find customizing step for the first 3 options, they disappeared.

In the table TMVF are maintained- SM30 did not help.

entry.title: Is there a way to get my PM orders with a basic start & finish dates 1 month apart?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Is there a way to get my scheduled preventive maintenance orders having basic start date at the beginning of the month and the basic finish at the end of the month? I plan to have this automated for all work orders.

entry.title: BOM component not showing in PI Sheet but shows on process order
entry.content:
Hi Experts.

The new BOM component is not showing in CO60 (PI Sheet) but shows on process order (COR3). Please help. Existing BOM components appear, but when I try to add a new component to the BOM it doesn't pull through to to the PI sheet.

entry.title: How to check the socket communication log
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Check the connection status between SAP and external devices at the socket communication handshake level.
Could you tell me how to check the logs (transactions)?
For example:
socket->bind()->listen()->connect()->accept()
at the log level.
If available, the transaction code and the log name to be referenced
I would appreciate it if you could tell me.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Any standard program to update AFBN without editing standard Tables
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is there any standard SAP program we've to perform AFBN without editing standard table in debug mode?

Currently we're performing this activity in debug mode after updating custom table -
Table - T093A - will be updated for CoD -->90--> from 00 to 30 (WRTAFB) --> AFBN to activate area 90,91 and 92 --> Go back to T093A and update from 30 (old) to 00

Table - T093B - will be updated for companies, ABGJA  for dep area 90,91 and 92

Note - These are the new depreciation area we've created as a part of Turkey Inflation Asset Accounting.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Five Key assessments for a Smooth ECC to S/4HANA Transformation
entry.content:
Greetings to all!           

      Embarking on the journey from an existing SAP ERP system to SAP S/4HANA can be a complex and daunting task. The transition involves assessing the current landscape, understanding the implications of the move, and preparing for the changes ahead. In the initial phase of this transformation, focusing on key strategies can help streamline the process and set the stage for a successful transition. Here, we discuss five essential assessments that organizations can do to navigate this transformation effectively, ensuring minimal disruption and maximum benefit.

Also when initiating a system conversion project, time and effort estimation are crucial to ensure the project stays on track. However, achieving accurate estimations can be challenging without data-driven actions.

To simplify this process, we can perform five key assessments:

  1. Business Transformation Assessment
  2. Landscape Assessment
  3. Process Assessment
  4. Custom Objects Assessment
  5. Timeline Assessment

Let's get started

1. Business Transformation Assessment

Without a proper understanding of the landscape and the knowledge of how each of the processes is affected, it is literally impossible to build a proper digital transformation plan.

Collect all the business processes from the legacy system and organize them hierarchically into the following levels

L1 – Line of business

L2 – Process group

L3 – Scope item

L4 – Process variant

L5 – Process step

And conduct a materiality analysis of all the Functional Modules. This will help you identify the heavily used modules and the rarely used modules in the existing landscape. The primary purpose of this analysis is to understand the AS-IS state.

2. Landscape Assessment

 2.1  Active vs Inactive Users
         Find out the total number of SAP ERP users and classify them into active and inactive users based on their activity. As a result, this section gives you an analytical view of all the users present in the system and helps you make the right decision with respect to license purchase before moving to S/4HANA.

 2.2 Add-on Analytics   

        Your transition from ECC to S/4HANA can be hindered by an incompatible add-on. All add-ons must be certified to run on S/4HANA. For SAP's own add-ons or those sold through SAP's pricelist, you can directly request the status of add-ons and additional packages from SAP. However, SAP is not responsible for independent third-party add-ons.

 2.3 Archiving List

       Archiving in SAP refers to the process of moving data that is no longer actively used or needed for daily operations to a separate storage location. This helps reduce the size of the production database, improve system performance, and ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements regarding data retention.

3. Process Assessment

 3.1 Enterprise Structure Mining

    Enterprise Structure Mining assessments are crucial for understanding the current organizational setup, including the number of company codes, sales organizations, purchase organizations, and other key elements. This information is used to identify opportunities for streamlining processes, improving efficiency, and aligning the SAP system with the organization's strategic objectives.

For example, in the case of Sales documents, the analysis drills down from company codes, sales organizations, distribution channels, plants, and all the way up to the sales documents.                                     

 3.2 Complexity Analysis

     Analyze the complexity of the system in terms of parameters such as the New G/L, Special Purpose Ledger, Material Ledger, Multi-Currency model, and Business Partners that are active in the existing landscape.

 

 3.3 Simplification Item Check

       Simplification analysis is a crucial step in preparing for an SAP S/4HANA conversion. It involves assessing the potential adaptation work needed to transition from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA at a functional level. This analysis helps identify simplification items, which are changes or adaptations required for business processes and custom developments to align with SAP S/4HANA's functionalities and best practices. The analysis provides insights into the impact of these changes, helping organizations plan and execute their conversion projects effectively.

 3.4  Training Plan

        In a system conversion project like transitioning to SAP S/4HANA, it's crucial to prioritize training for end-users. Often, the focus is on the technical aspects of the project, but neglecting user training can lead to challenges post-implementation. To ensure a successful transition, create a comprehensive training plan that includes scenario-based training and onboard end-users early in the process. This approach not only prepares users for the new system but also helps in embracing a mindset shift towards leveraging SAP S/4HANA for success in the digital economy.

4. Custom Objects Assessment

     In the Custom Objects Assessment, the goal is to identify all custom objects in the system. These custom objects may not be readable in the S/4HANA system, potentially requiring significant time for adaptation. By the end of this assessment, you should have the total number of custom objects, modules with a high number of custom objects, total impacted objects, and total impacted cases. This information is crucial for planning and executing the transition to SAP S/4HANA effectively.

5. Timeline/Effort Assessment

 5.1  Timeline Assessment

Timeline Assessment involves planning the project timeline based on team size, assessment results, and project scope. It includes creating a high-level roadmap for the SAP S/4HANA system conversion process, validating it with solution architects and project managers, and charting out a detailed plan for the conversion project. This step is crucial for setting realistic deadlines, milestones, and sub-phase timelines to ensure the project stays on track and is completed successfully.  

 5.2 Effort Estimation

 Effort Estimation in the context of SAP S/4HANA conversion refers to accurately predicting the resources and efforts required for the conversion project. It involves identifying the necessary preparation and pre-checks in the current system landscape, estimating HANA database and hardware sizing, and quantifying the IT effort needed for the transition. This helps in optimizing the total cost of conversion and deploying the right resources for a successful SAP S/4HANA realization.

entry.title: Cost center report by period
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for a cost center report which was used in the last year but I can´t find in the SAP Menu. 

It shows a cost center split by period and with quarterly totals. The report needs to be with cost elements.

Excel Download:

 

BR

Tobias

entry.title: Merging Customer & Vendor into single BP
entry.content:
Hello,

During CVI pre-conversion activity on ECC/ERP system, If we want to merge customers and vendors into single BP which are having same legal entity (address) then in customization path do we need to assign customer & vendor account group to the same BP Grouping? Not all customers & vendors from those account groups are eligible to merge into single BP.

Thank you

entry.title: COR6N Yield UoM
entry.content:
Hi All, 

In the material master, Base Unit is BT (Bottle) and the conversion is defined between PAL and BT.    

Master recipe is defined with the same BT unit only and Operation/Phase unit is also BT.  

During Process Order confirmation [COR6N] as soon as I select order number and Phase number then the wrong Unit of measure for Yield is populated by-default. (e.g. PAL for palette) 

Now, If I manually delete this default unit from the COR6N screen and when I press the enter key then the correct unit (e.g. BT for bottle) is getting appeared from the master recipe's operation. 

Question is why COR6N screen picks up PAL unit by default?

entry.title: Mass Change Email Recipient for Jobs
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to set the email recipient for all jobs of the SM37 to a single email address. Is there a way to enter the email address en masse or can this only be done manually?

Thank you

entry.title: How to get SQL Server 2012 installation media
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We are planning to build new MSSQL server environment with production backup. As production system is having the version details as below.

Details :

Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio            11.0.DIGITS.0
Microsoft Analysis Services Client Tools            11.0.DIGITS.0
Microsoft Data Access Components (MDAC)            10.0.ZIPCODE.DIGITS
Microsoft MSXML            3.0 6.0 
Microsoft Internet Explorer            9.11.ZIPCODE.0
Microsoft .NET Framework            4.0.ZIPCODE.ZIPCODE
Operating System            6.3.ZIPCODE

 

SQL_Latin1_General_CP850_BIN2

We are already checked in SAP market place the MSSQL is starting from 2014, so can kindly provide the MSSQL version 2012.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Find out the table name
entry.content:
Hi,
i wanted to find out the table name.
so i did the transaction and went to the column i needed and clicked on it and then pressed F1. but under the field data there is only "Data element " is given , no table name . what should i do to get the bale name . I dont have acces to SE11 transaction .
what are the ways to get the table name ?

entry.title: What configuration needs to be done either in ECC(S4) or EWM side to create ID in EWM, skipping IDN?
entry.content:
Hello, 

What configuration needs to be done either in ECC(S4) or EWM side to create Inbound delivery directly in EWM, skipping the need for Inbound delivery notification document?

 

Regards

Bharath

entry.title: Under-delivery return order
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have created a return delivery for 6 hus. In practice, we see that only 5 pallets have been returned by our customer. We started processing the return delivery for the 5 hus which are returned. Therefore we do have a picking and packing status of B. The delivery quantity is grayed out and therefore the delivery quantity cannot be adjusted anymore.

I thought that this can be solved by unassigning the hus in the packing stage and then adjusting the quantity. However unassigning the hus is not possible as those are already picked. Do you have any tips/ suggestions on how to process an under-delivery for a return delivery while the delivery is already partially processed? Any help is much appreciated!

entry.title: SAP Logon can't be started correctly - SAPUILandscape.xml - environment variable SAPLOGON_LSXML_FILE
entry.content:
I have a user who gets this error every time they restart or shut down their laptop:

SAP Logon cannot be started correctly.

Error while loading configuration file:

*redacted*\SAPUILandscape.xml

defined by environment variable SAPLOGON_LSXML_FILE

Adjust the input file name and ensure that the file is available and has the correct format

Just trying to figure out how to resolve this error every time they restart their laptop. Admittedly, I'm new to supporting SAP, so any help would be appreciated.

entry.title: Archive order confirmation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Have a legal requirement requiring to archive the various order confirmations generated in modification and in initial creation of Sales Orders.

Any solution on SAP ECC6  ? 

Thank you

entry.title: Winshuttle script execution - Error: Field is not an input field
entry.content:
I want to record a script in VA01 in Winshuttle Studio. Therefore I need to adjust the item conditions. For this I have to manually overwrite the automatically stored price with the Price Condition PR10. During the recording there are no problems but as soon as I want to execute the script I get the error message:

Field (KOMV-KWERT Condition value) (6) SAPMV45A 5003 is not an input field

entry.title: Asset transfer ABUMN - partial transfer of wrongly added acquisition value
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts

I have a question regarding the partial transfer of an asset using ABUMN.

In 2019 an asset was created (laptop).  Use life : 3 years.

In 2023 (asset is already fully depreciated – NBV = zero), a new asset was created for the replacement of this old asset.  But when posting the invoice, the old asset was used instead of the new one.  So the acquisition value on the old asset increased with the value of the new asset.  But because the initial asset was already fully depreciated, the new value did not depreciate.

Now I want to transfer the new value to the new created asset.  How can I do this (partial) transfer ?  I only want to transfer the new value to the new asset and keep the original value on the old asset.

When I try to do the partial transfer, using the new value, also a part of the accumulated deprecation will be transferred and that is not the purpose.  I only want to transfer the new acquisition value, which has not been depreciated yet.  Original acquisition value and accumulated depreciation (same amount because fully depreciated) should remain on the old asset.

Thank you for your input !  Much appreciated !
